invariants.statement: A failure inside Tracer or a Storage backend must never propagate into the traced agent's own control flow. Tracer already catches and saves a partial trace on exception (core/tracer.py); any new instrumentation path (adapters, new backends) must preserve this — the thing being observed must survive the observer breaking.
invariants.status: held for core+openai adapter; not yet verified for hypothetical future adapters or storage backends

invariants.statement: Traces store prompts, tool arguments, and tool results verbatim and unencrypted (core/storage.py, adapters/openai.py) because redaction would defeat the tool's purpose of showing exactly what happened. This is a deliberate trust-boundary decision, not an oversight — but it means any code path that could expose a .db file over the network (viewer/server.py) or widen what gets written must treat that as a security-relevant change, not a routine one.
invariants.status: held, but no redaction hook exists yet (tracked as CHANGELOG known limitation) — this is the sharpest edge in the project

invariants.statement: adapters/openai.py never imports the `openai` package and reads responses structurally (dict-like access), which is why it also drives xAI/Groq/Ollama over the same wire format. Any new adapter code must preserve this — importing a provider SDK directly for type-checking would silently break provider portability.
